title: Bitter Juicy Dutch Chicken Roast
description: A single-serving Dutch chicken roast that balances the bright bitterness of grapefruit with rich juices, cooked sousvide for tenderness and finished in a quick sear for depth.
instructions: Season the chicken breast with salt, pepper, crushed garlic, and rosemary sprigs.
Place the seasoned chicken in a vacuum‑seal bag with the grapefruit juice and 1 tbsp of olive oil.
Vacuum seal the bag and preheat a sous‑vide water bath to 65 °C (149 °F).
Cook the sealed chicken in the water bath for 60 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the bag, pat it dry with paper towels to ensure a crisp sear.
Heat a skillet over medium‑high heat, add 1 tbsp olive oil and the butter.
Sear the chicken breast for 2–3 minutes per side until golden brown and caramelized.
Let the seared chicken rest for 3 minutes before serving.
Slice and serve immediately, garnishing with a fresh rosemary sprig if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
